posts.headline: Sherwani hunger strike timed to coincide with Lahur Sheikh Jangi protest, framed as demand for equal legal treatment
posts.summary: Reads imprisoned journalist Sherwan Sherwani's new hunger strike as a deliberate signal, timing it with Kurdish journalism day and the parallel Lahur Sheikh Jangi action to pressure PUK and KRG institutions toward a consistent rule-of-law standard.

posts.headline: Tanker convoys seen leaving Khor Mor gas field heading toward the Iranian border
posts.summary: Flags a convoy of tankers recorded travelling from Chamchamal, where the Khor Mor gas field sits, toward the Iranian border, alleging product is being diverted into private sales with PUK-linked and possibly KDP-linked beneficiaries.

posts.headline: Kurdistan Region men surfacing as fighters in the Russian army after southern Iraq recruitment pipeline
posts.summary: Documents another KRG resident whose family has had no contact for more than six months now appearing to fight with Russian forces, pointing to a southern Iraqi intermediary network recruiting on employment and high-salary promises.

posts.headline: Trump statement says Iran is struggling to identify a single leader to negotiate with
posts.summary: Quotes a Trump line framing Tehran as lacking a clear interlocutor on the Iranian side of any talks, reinforcing the US line that Iranian decision-making is fragmented in the aftermath of the war. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Iraqi parliament decides to send a delegation to Erbil as PM formation clock runs down
posts.summary: Flags a parliament resolution to dispatch a formal delegation to Erbil, read as an attempt to lock in KDP and KRG sign-off on the Coordination Framework PM pick before the April 26 constitutional deadline. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: US embassy in Baghdad reported to be upgrading defensive posture amid militia threats
posts.summary: Reports physical and security upgrades at the US embassy compound in Baghdad, consistent with the April 20 State Department alert on Iran-aligned militia attack plotting. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Parliamentary presidency agrees to extend the current legislative session
posts.summary: Flags a deal between the parliamentary presidency and bloc leaders to extend the current session, buying procedural room for a PM nomination vote inside the constitutional window. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Citing Oil Price report, lays out Iraqi militia financial networks and oil-smuggling revenue lines
posts.summary: Paraphrases an Oil Price investigation on how PMF-aligned factions monetise oil diversion, real estate, and border crossings, framing it as the hard economic spine that makes political leverage possible. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: US Department of War announces seizure of an Iranian oil tanker
posts.summary: Reports a US military seizure of an Iranian-flagged tanker, consistent with the ongoing Hormuz enforcement posture and the broader pressure campaign on Iranian crude revenue. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Warns a US–Iran scenario would be worse for Iraq than the 1990s sanctions siege
posts.summary: Argues that given cross-border trade, currency exposure, and militia integration, any sustained US–Iran economic war would hit Iraq harder than the 1990s UN sanctions regime. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: New rise in parallel-market dollar rate in Iraq
posts.summary: Flags a fresh uptick in the parallel-market dollar rate against the dinar, putting pressure back on CBI's dollar auction controls and the Oliver Wyman-era private-bank reform sequencing. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: US mission in Iraq escalating pressure on Iraqi political leaders
posts.summary: Reports direct US engagement with Coordination Framework leaders on the PM choice and on militia-linked cabinet portfolios, framed as explicit conditionality rather than diplomatic suggestion. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: JP Morgan reportedly halts certain transfer flows tied to Iraq
posts.summary: Flags a JP Morgan move to pause specific transfer categories connected to Iraqi counterparties, a signal that correspondent-banking risk appetite is tightening again as US secondary-sanctions posture hardens. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Quotes reaction to a reported Iraqi financial loss figure as a "terrible number"
posts.summary: Quote-tweets @onenewsiq with a reaction to a headline loss figure, flagging it as large enough to reshape the 2026 fiscal conversation. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Politico report said to detail Trump administration plans to punish Iraq over Iran ties
posts.summary: Paraphrases a Politico piece outlining specific US levers being drawn up to punish Iraqi actors for continuing Iran cooperation, including sanctions designations and trade-finance restrictions. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Japan's PM asked Saudi Arabia for expanded crude supplies
posts.summary: Breaks a Japanese request to Riyadh for incremental Saudi crude volumes, a signal that Asian importers are pre-positioning for a prolonged Hormuz disruption rather than betting on a quick normalisation.

posts.headline: QatarEnergy celebrates first LNG export cargo from the Golden Pass project
posts.summary: Flags the inaugural Golden Pass LNG cargo as a milestone that shifts Qatari LNG flows toward the US Gulf and reshapes the 2026 Atlantic Basin supply map against a tight Hormuz backdrop.

posts.headline: Detail thread on Qatar's first US-Gulf LNG cargo from Golden Pass
posts.summary: Breaks down the commercial and diplomatic significance of QatarEnergy exporting its first Golden Pass cargo, arguing it cements Doha as a bi-Atlantic LNG exporter and reframes US LNG geopolitics.

posts.headline: Retweets Argus Straits Talk on week 9 of the Hormuz conflict and partial transit restart
posts.summary: Signal-boosts an Argus Media segment unpacking the ceasefire-vs-dare dynamic on Hormuz, with tanker transit still partial and conditional passage becoming a structural premium.

posts.headline: Retweets German Chancellor welcoming an extension of the US–Iran ceasefire
posts.summary: Boosts the German chancellery statement welcoming the ceasefire extension, framing it as the first European-level political endorsement of the current pause.

posts.headline: Retweets Oman Energy Ministry statement denying PetroTel Energy discovery claims
posts.summary: Signal-boosts the Omani Energy Ministry's official statement pushing back on PetroTel Energy's public discovery claims, a rare public denial that recalibrates the Oman upstream narrative.

posts.headline: Reads current US naval posture as the start of a Hormuz super-FON effort
posts.summary: Argues the latest US vessel movements and the seized-ship disposition debate mark the beginning of a Hormuz super Freedom of Navigation posture, not a one-off convoy, with multi-year implications for Gulf shipping and insurance.

posts.headline: Retweets Konrad arguing the US should transfer seized Iranian ships to the Merchant Marine
posts.summary: Endorses a thread pushing for seized Iranian vessels to be transferred to the US Merchant Marine rather than auctioned, a move that would harden US sealift and compound the precedent value of the seizures.

posts.headline: Flags sodium perchlorate as the key word Gulf states need to raise with China
posts.summary: Argues that any GCC capital raising Chinese supply-chain concerns should anchor the conversation on sodium perchlorate, a precursor tied to Iran's missile program, rather than generic technology-transfer talking points.

posts.headline: Thread on what stands out in the war just concluded
posts.summary: Opens a longer analytical thread on the just-ended Iran war, flagging Iranian command-and-control degradation, GCC air defence interoperability, and the durability of the US–Gulf partnership as the three things worth rereading the war through.
